response_text:
# Git Branching Strategies: GitFlow vs Trunk-Based Development

## Core Philosophy Difference

```
GitFlow: Multiple long-lived branches, structured releases
Trunk-Based: Single main branch, continuous integration
```

---

## GitFlow

### Branch Structure
```
main (production)
├── develop (integration)
│   ├── feature/user-auth
│   ├── feature/payment-system
│   └── feature/dashboard
├── release/1.2.0
├── release/1.3.0
└── hotfix/critical-bug
```

### Branch Types & Purposes
```
main        → Production-ready code only
develop     → Integration branch for features
feature/*   → Individual features (branch from develop)
release/*   → Release preparation (branch from develop)
hotfix/*    → Emergency production fixes (branch from main)
```

### Typical GitFlow Lifecycle
```bash
# 1. Start a feature
git checkout develop
git checkout -b feature/user-authentication

# 2. Work on feature
git add .
git commit -m "Add login endpoint"
git commit -m "Add JWT token handling"

# 3. Merge feature back to develop
git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff feature/user-authentication
git branch -d feature/user-authentication

# 4. Create release branch
git checkout -b release/1.2.0

# 5. Bug fixes on release branch
git commit -m "Fix login edge case"
git commit -m "Update version number"

# 6. Finish release - merge to BOTH main and develop
git checkout main
git merge --no-ff release/1.2.0
git tag -a v1.2.0 -m "Release version 1.2.0"

git checkout develop
git merge --no-ff release/1.2.0
git branch -d release/1.2.0

# 7. Hotfix if needed
git checkout main
git checkout -b hotfix/payment-crash
git commit -m "Fix null pointer in payment"

git checkout main
git merge --no-ff hotfix/payment-crash
git tag -a v1.2.1

git checkout develop
